description:
You are a FinOps analyst who wants to test-drive a tiny Python toolkit for analysing cloud-cost data.  
    Complete the following end-to-end workflow entirely from the terminal:

    1. Create a new work directory called  
       /home/user/cloud_finops  

    2. Inside that directory create a plain-text file named  
       /home/user/cloud_finops/requirements.txt  
       The file must contain **exactly** the three lines shown below, in this order, with no leading or trailing spaces:  
           boto3==1.34.105  
           numpy==1.26.4  
           pandas==2.2.2  

    3. Create an isolated Python virtual environment in the same directory.  
       • The folder that holds the virtual environment must be called /home/user/cloud_finops/finops_env.  
       • Use only standard Python tooling that is available on an ordinary Linux machine (virtualenv or the built-in venv module are both fine).  

    4. Activate the virtual environment and install every package from requirements.txt.  
       No optional extras or upgrades are required—just the three exact versions listed.  

    5. While the virtual environment is still active, produce a freeze log so stakeholders can verify the exact state of the environment.  
       • Run a command that outputs the fully frozen dependency list and redirect the result to  
         /home/user/cloud_finops/installation_log.txt  
       • The log file must follow the canonical “pip freeze” format: one package per line in the form NAME==VERSION.  
       • The file must contain at least the three mandatory lines for boto3, numpy and pandas.  
         Additional transitive dependencies (e.g. botocore, python-dateutil, etc.) are acceptable and expected, but every line must follow the same NAME==VERSION pattern.  
       • For automated grading the file must be plain ASCII, end each line with a single newline “\n”, and have no blank lines at the top or bottom.

    What the grader will check:
    • Directory /home/user/cloud_finops exists.  
    • Directory /home/user/cloud_finops/finops_env exists and looks like a Python virtual environment (bin/activate must be present).  
    • File /home/user/cloud_finops/requirements.txt exists and matches the three-line specification **exactly**.  
    • File /home/user/cloud_finops/installation_log.txt exists and contains lines for  
        boto3==1.34.105  
        numpy==1.26.4  
        pandas==2.2.2  

    Do NOT hard-code absolute paths in the freeze log; the pip tool will handle that automatically.  
    Perform the steps using normal, non-sudo Linux commands available in a minimal container.
truth:
Required artefacts after successful completion:

    1. Directory /home/user/cloud_finops/
       • Must exist with standard user permissions (rwx for user).

    2. File /home/user/cloud_finops/requirements.txt  
       Exact byte sequence (including trailing newline):  
           "boto3==1.34.105\nnumpy==1.26.4\npandas==2.2.2\n"

    3. Virtual environment directory /home/user/cloud_finops/finops_env  
       • Must contain bin/activate (or Scripts/activate on Windows, but Linux container is assumed).  
       • Python inside the venv must be able to import boto3, numpy, pandas.

    4. File /home/user/cloud_finops/installation_log.txt  
       • Must be produced by `pip freeze` executed from inside finops_env.  
       • Minimal expected subset (order does NOT matter as long as alphabetical; grader will search for these exact lines):  
           boto3==1.34.105  
           numpy==1.26.4  
           pandas==2.2.2  
       • Additional lines such as botocore==...  jmespath==...  etc. are allowed.

    The automated test will:
       a) cat /home/user/cloud_finops/requirements.txt and verify exact match.  
       b) grep each of the three required package/version pairs inside installation_log.txt.  
       c) test -f /home/user/cloud_finops/finops_env/bin/activate.
